title: Reproducible Local Environment: Docker Compose for Discovery, Sentinel, Database, Cache, and Blockchain Devnet
description: Create a fully functional local development environment using Docker Compose. All platform components must start with a single 'docker compose up' command. This includes PostgreSQL, Redis, Discovery Service, Sentinel (Producer + Consumer), a local blockchain node (Hardhat or Anvil), and an OpenTelemetry Collector. A seed script deploys smart contracts to the local chain and populates the database for development.
objectives: Complete local environment with one command
Reproducible: running 'make reset' destroys all state and restarts cleanly
Environment variables clearly separated from secrets — use .env.local for dev secrets only
All service health-checks configured — compose waits for dependencies before starting dependent services

implementation_guidance.overview: Use Docker Compose v2 (compose.yaml, not docker-compose.yaml for newer versions). Define a shared network 'sentinel_net'. Each service has a healthcheck. Use depends_on: condition: service_healthy. Use named volumes for persistence. Provide a seed.py script that deploys contracts to the local Hardhat/Anvil node and inserts bootstrap data into PostgreSQL.
steps: Step 1 - PostgreSQL Service: image postgres:15-alpine. Environment: POSTGRES_USER=sentinel_user, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=sentinel_pass, POSTGRES_DB=sentinel_db. Healthcheck: pg_isready -U sentinel_user -d sentinel_db. Volume: postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data. Port: 5432:5432.

Step 2 - Redis Service: image redis:7-alpine with --requirepass flag (password: redis_secret_dev). Healthcheck: redis-cli -a redis_secret_dev ping. Port: 6379:6379. Volume: redis_data:/data. Enable AOF persistence: redis-server --appendonly yes --requirepass redis_secret_dev.

Step 3 - Local Blockchain Node: Use hardhat node from the /contracts directory OR use ghcr.io/foundry-rs/foundry:latest (Anvil). Preferred: ghcr.io/foundry-rs/foundry with command: anvil --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8545 --chain-id 31337 --block-time 1. Healthcheck: curl -sf http://localhost:8545 with JSON-RPC eth_chainId call. Port: 8545:8545.

Step 4 - OTel Collector: Use otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib:0.100.0. Mount config: ./deploy/otel-collector-config.yaml:/etc/otelcol-contrib/config.yaml. Listens on 4317 (gRPC OTLP) and 4318 (HTTP OTLP). Exports to Prometheus on port 8889. Writes logs to stdout.

Step 5 - Discovery Service: Build from ./services/discovery/Dockerfile. Environment: from .env.local plus: DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://sentinel_user:sentinel_pass@postgres:5432/sentinel_db, REDIS_URL=redis://:redis_secret_dev@redis:6379/0, BLOCKCHAIN_RPC_URL=http://hardhat:8545, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector:4318, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=discovery-service. depends_on: postgres (service_healthy), redis (service_healthy). Healthcheck: curl -f http://localhost:8000/health. Port: 8000:8000.

Step 6 - Sentinel Producer: Build from ./services/sentinel/Dockerfile. Environment: SENTINEL_ROLE=producer, SERVICE_ID=dev-sentinel-alpha, SENTINEL_ENV=dev, DATABASE_URL (same as discovery), REDIS_URL, BLOCKCHAIN_RPC_URL (hardhat), OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=sentinel-producer, SECRET_STORAGE_BACKEND=local_file, SECRET_STORAGE_KEY_FILE=/data/sentinel.key.enc. Mount volume: sentinel_producer_data:/data. depends_on: discovery (service_healthy), hardhat (service_healthy). Port: 8100:8000.
Step 7 - Sentinel Consumer: Same as producer but SENTINEL_ROLE=consumer, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=sentinel-consumer, volume sentinel_consumer_data:/data. Port: 8101:8000.

Step 8 - Seed Script (scripts/seed.py): Run after all services are healthy. Phase 1 (Chain): Connect to http://localhost:8545 via web3.py. Deploy IssuerRegistry, TrustPolicyRegistry, StatusRegistry, ServiceRegistry contracts using the first Hardhat/Anvil pre-funded account. Save deployed addresses to contracts/deployments/local.json. Phase 2 (DB): Connect to PostgreSQL via psycopg2 or asyncpg. Insert a bootstrap issuer record, a test service record, and sample VC data for development. Phase 3 (Sentinel): Generate a test enrollment token via the Discovery Service API and trigger onboarding of the dev sentinel instances.
Step 9 - Reset Script (scripts/reset.sh): docker compose down -v (removes all volumes). docker compose up -d. Wait for healthy state. python scripts/seed.py. This must complete within 120 seconds.

Step 10 - OTel Collector Configuration (deploy/otel-collector-config.yaml): receivers: otlp (grpc:4317, http:4318). processors: batch. exporters: prometheus (endpoint 0.0.0.0:8889), debug (verbosity: normal). service.pipelines: traces (otlp → batch → debug), metrics (otlp → batch → prometheus), logs (otlp → batch → debug).

Step 11 - .env.local.example: Create this file (committed) with all required environment variable names and placeholder values. Real .env.local (ignored by git) is created by developer. Required vars: POSTGRES_PASSWORD, REDIS_PASSWORD, HARDHAT_PRIVATE_KEY (first Hardhat account), SECRET_STORAGE_MASTER_KEY (AES-256 hex).

Step 12 - Healthcheck Design: All services must expose /health endpoint returning HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status": "ok", "service": "<name>", "version": "<semver>"}. Docker healthcheck uses interval: 10s, timeout: 5s, retries: 5, start_period: 30s.

scripts/.seed.py: Deploy contracts + insert bootstrap DB data

seed_output_local_json.description: contracts/deployments/local.json written by seed.py after contract deployment

security_constraints: .env.local MUST be in .gitignore — never commit real credentials
All containers run as non-root user (uid 1000) — Dockerfiles must include USER 1000
PostgreSQL password must be at least 16 characters in local dev — enforced by seed script validation
Hardhat/Anvil local node is only exposed on localhost — never bind to 0.0.0.0 in CI without firewall
No default TLS in local compose — TLS tested only in staging/prod environments
Sentinel key encryption key (SECRET_STORAGE_MASTER_KEY) must be a 32-byte hex string — seed script generates and stores in .env.local if not present

technical_requirements: Docker Compose v2 (docker compose, not docker-compose)
All services must start and be healthy within 90 seconds on first run (excluding image pull time)
make reset completes within 120 seconds (after images are built)
Tested on Linux and macOS; Windows via WSL2
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up -d starts all services
docker compose ps shows all services as healthy within 90 seconds
python scripts/seed.py completes without errors after docker compose up
make reset destroys all volumes and restores a clean functional state
curl http://localhost:8545 with eth_chainId JSON-RPC call returns chainId 31337
No default passwords or secrets are committed to the repository
